body: Closing as duplicate of #714, which covers the same task (creating an OpenClaw/ClawHub skill package) with complete acceptance criteria and affected files. All work should proceed under #714.
Closing as duplicate of #715, which covers the same task (publishing to ClawHub) with complete acceptance criteria and affected files. All work should proceed under #715.

## Context

After ClawHub publishing (#715), expand reach by listing on secondary registries and discovery channels.

## Dependencies
- #715 (ClawHub listing must be live first)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] disinto skill appears in SkillsMP search (auto-indexed from GitHub or submitted manually)
- [ ] PR submitted to awesome-agent-skills repo listing disinto under DevOps/Automation
- [ ] SkillHub listing submitted (or confirmed live)
- [ ] GitHub repo topics updated: `agent-skill`, `openclaw`, `clawhub`, `code-factory`, `automation`

## Affected files
- `README.md` (add secondary registry badges/links)
- `.github/` or repo settings (GitHub topics — manual step)

## Action items

### SkillsMP (skillsmp.com)
- [ ] SkillsMP auto-indexes from GitHub — ensure the skill directory is in the public repo
- [ ] Verify disinto appears in SkillsMP search after a few days
- [ ] If not auto-indexed, submit manually

### awesome-agent-skills
- [ ] Submit PR to github.com/skillmatic-ai/awesome-agent-skills
- [ ] Add disinto under appropriate category (DevOps / Automation)

### SkillHub (skillhub.club)
- [ ] Submit skill for AI evaluation
- [ ] Verify listing

### LobeHub (lobehub.com/skills)
- [ ] Submit skill to curated directory

### GitHub discoverability
- [ ] Add topics to repo: `agent-skill`, `openclaw`, `clawhub`, `code-factory`, `automation`
- [ ] Ensure SKILL.md is discoverable at repo root or skill/ directory

## References

- Research report: #709
- Skill package: #714
- ClawHub listing: #715

title: fix: add weekly Docker prune cron to prevent recurring disk P1 threshold breach
body:
## Problem

Disk has twice crossed the 80% P1 threshold in two days (peaked at 82% on 2026-03-24). The supervisor performs reactive Docker prune when P1 is hit, but a proactive scheduled cleanup would prevent the threshold from being crossed in the first place.

Supersedes prediction in #644.

## Action

Add a scheduled weekly Docker prune to the supervisor or cron config so Docker image/container buildup is cleared before it reaches crisis levels. The cleanup should run at a time offset from the 06:00 formula burst (e.g. Sunday 04:00 UTC).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A Docker prune cron entry (weekly, off-peak) is added to the factory cron config or supervisor schedule
- [ ] The cron runs `docker system prune -f && docker image prune -f --filter "until=168h"` (keeping images used in last 7 days)
- [ ] Cron time does not overlap with the 06:00 formula burst (gardener, predictor, supervisor)
- [ ] After the change, disk stays below 75% between reactive supervisor prune events

## Affected files
- `supervisor/supervisor-run.sh` (if adding to supervisor schedule)
- or cron config file (if adding standalone cron entry)
- `projects/disinto.toml.example` (if scheduling parameters belong there)

## Related
- #644 (prediction that triggered this)

Actioned: created a backlog issue for adding a proactive weekly Docker prune cron to prevent recurring P1 disk threshold breaches. This supplements the reactive supervisor cleanup with a scheduled preventive pass.

reason: prediction actioned — recurring Docker prune cron backlog issue created

title: fix: upgrade Caddy to v2.11.1 on harb-staging to patch CVE-2026-27590 RCE and medium CVEs
body:
## Problem

Three CVEs affecting Caddy are fixed in v2.11.1:
- CVE-2026-27590 (HIGH — RCE via FastCGI path-splitting bug)
- Two medium CVEs (see #580 for details)

harb-staging is currently running an older Caddy version and needs an upgrade.

Supersedes prediction in #580.

## Action

Upgrade Caddy to v2.11.1 on harb-staging. Verify the service restarts cleanly.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `caddy version` on harb-staging shows v2.11.1 or later
- [ ] Caddy service is running and serving requests after upgrade
- [ ] No CVE-2026-27590, CVE-2026-27589 in installed version

## Affected files
- harb-staging host: `/usr/local/bin/caddy` (upgraded in-place or via package manager)

## References
- Prediction: #580
- CVE advisory: CVE-2026-27590 (FastCGI RCE), CVE-2026-27589 (medium)
Actioned: created an action issue for upgrading Caddy to v2.11.1 on harb-staging to remediate CVE-2026-27590 (HIGH/RCE) and two medium CVEs. Priority: high.

reason: prediction actioned — Caddy upgrade action issue created

title: fix: stagger formula agent cron start times to reduce simultaneous 06:00 RAM burst
body:
## Problem

Three formula agents (gardener, predictor, supervisor) all start at 06:00 UTC simultaneously, competing for RAM and driving swap usage to 57% (2335MB / 4095MB). Swap spikes under the burst introduce latency and risk OOM events if usage grows further.

Supersedes prediction in #529.

## Action

Stagger the cron start times for formula agents by 1–2 minutes each so RAM pressure is distributed across time rather than concentrated at 06:00.

Suggested schedule:
- Supervisor: 06:00 (runs first, gathers health snapshot)
- Predictor: 06:02
- Gardener: 06:04
- Planner: 06:06 (if on same schedule)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Formula agent cron entries are offset by at least 1 minute from each other
- [ ] No two formula agents start within the same minute
- [ ] Swap usage at the 06:00–06:10 window stays below 50% after the change

## Affected files
- `projects/disinto.toml` (cron schedule fields, if stored there)
- or host cron config file (e.g. `/etc/cron.d/disinto-*`)
- `BOOTSTRAP.md` (update documented cron schedule if shown there)

## Related
- #529 (prediction that triggered this)

Actioned: created a backlog issue for staggering formula agent cron start times (supervisor 06:00, predictor 06:02, gardener 06:04) to distribute the RAM burst across 4–6 minutes instead of hitting simultaneously.

reason: prediction actioned — cron stagger backlog issue created
